title: ServeMux Patterns, Precedence, Path Values, and Route Safety · Program Defense
summary: A production-readiness review compares near-miss decisions for servemux patterns, precedence, path values, and route safety and requires the smallest safe validated ServeMux route and precedence table with changed-request evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for validated ServeMux route and precedence table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Author Go 1.26 ServeMux patterns with optional method, host, literal segments, single wildcards, remainder wildcards, and end markers.
Derive most-specific precedence as request-set inclusion and detect conflicting patterns before startup.
Use Request.PathValue while distinguishing segment unescaping, escaped slash data, raw query, cleaned paths, and invalid identifiers.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Route Registration Gate
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz team handles case 9695 in a partner API with an outdated generated client. Build deterministic Go evidence to choose a specific method-and-path route and preserve one registration owner; then remove one trusted intermediary and recompute authority without forwarded fields. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compile route registration, recover only to report startup conflicts, enumerate method-path cases, and test near-miss patterns.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
